element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early combat encounters, foundational attack tool
Enhanced damage output, standard progression weapon
Ranged combat option, resource management (arrows limited)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Zelda-like structure alternating exploration, puzzle-solving, and combat encounters with story progression gates via duels

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Treasure chests hidden throughout levels requiring keys; side areas accessible after acquiring tools
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden locations in locations like haunted wells and catacombs suggest unconventional navigation/interaction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic knife combat and simple exploration (Catacomb, Arab jail exterior)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive weapon upgrades available; introduction of ranged combat and tool-based puzzles; duel encounters begin
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-difficulty duels, large boss encounters (Boar, Celts), final castle sequences and wedding ending

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured around named locations (Catacomb, Hadrian's Wall, Locksley Castle, Dubois Manor, etc.) that correspond to story beats; each location functions as a discrete level with combat and exploration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: CATACOMB (Arab jail exterior), LOCKSLEY (Locksley Castle), DUBOIS (Dubois Manor)

notable_level_patterns.description: Duels serve as mandatory progression gates between story areas; successful duel completion required to advance narrative
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple duel encounters leading to WEDDING (final ending)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels encourage exploration to find locked chests, healing items, and currency drops; inventory management becomes puzzle element
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All exploration areas; WELL (haunted well search), POND (healing water search)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression sequences with directional momentum (e.g., horse race to Sherwood, escape sequences)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: CHASE (Horse race to Sherwood), pursuit sequences in later areas

notable_level_patterns.description: Static or semi-mobile defense scenarios against waves of enemies

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves NES article)
